originalTitle: Middle East crisis live: tankers carrying Saudi oil turned back after Houthi threats; US launches more strikes on Iran
summary: In a letter to shippers, the Houthis have threatened to attack any ships that load or discharge Saudi oil
content: A little more detail on those US strikes: Iranian state media says air defence activity has been heard in western, eastern and north-eastern Tehran. The time in Tehran is 3.40am. To recap some news from earlier today, Donald Trump said the US ⁠will ⁠be hitting the Pickaxe Mountain area, ⁠located near Iran’s heavily damaged ⁠Natanz uranium enrichment facility, “pretty ‌soon”. It is unclear if today’s US strikes are targeting Pickaxe Mountain. Pickaxe ‌Mountain is a ‌heavily fortified site that hosts two deeply buried tunnel complexes ‌that experts assess as beyond the reach of the most powerful bunker ⁠buster bombs in the US arsenal. The site is linked ‌to Iran’s nuclear program. Iran’s military leaders said any attack on nuclear or “other sensitive facilities” would be considered a major escalation, according to a statement reported by Iranian state media. “We declare that if the invading and terrorist military of that country enters such a phase, we will regard it as an expansion of the war across the region, and all interests of the United States, its allies, and its supporters will become targets of powerful attacks by the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran,” said the statement from the armed forces’ Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters. US Central Command has announced it has begun striking military targets in Iran for the 11th consecutive night. The strikes are designed to continue degrading Iran’s ability to threaten commercial shipping in the strait of Hormuz, it said. We’ll bring you more details on these strikes as we learn more. The number of wounded US personnel in the Iran war has grown to well over 500 troops, a US official told the Associated Press. The total is higher than the Pentagon’s official tally on the Defense Casualty Analysis System, which currently shows 482 wounded. The US official, who was not authorised to discuss the matter with the media and spoke on condition of anonymity, said the system has a significant lag in reporting injuries. The official said that was a function of its design and not intentional. Pakistan’s prime minister Shehbaz Sharif called for restraint from all warring sides during a meeting with Iran’s interior minister on Tuesday. Sharif “urged all sides to exercise restraint and refrain from actions that could further destabilise the region”, a statement from his office said, adding that he told Iran’s Eskandar Momeni that Pakistan would continue to act as a mediator. Speaking from the Oval Office, Donald Trump gave a dim view on possible talks, saying the US had “no interest in meeting”. To recap, the Houthis, who control northern and western Yemen including the coast at the mouth of the Red Sea, announced a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia on ⁠Monday. The announcement already appears to be having an impact. Two tankers which had just loaded Saudi crude bound for China and India at Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Yanbu made U-turns on Tuesday, heading towards the Suez Canal rather ‌than out through the Bab el-Mandeb into the Indian Ocean. Assessing the situation, British maritime risk management group Vanguard said: The developments represent the first confirmed changes to commercial tanker routing following the (Houthi) embargo and are likely to increase disruption to Saudi crude exports and regional shipping patterns.” With the war shutting the strait of Hormuz, the Red Sea has served as the main alternate route out for millions of barrels of Saudi oil per day, diverted by pipeline through Yanbu. In case you’re just joining us, here’s the main developments to know: US forces bombed targets in the south and west of Iran overnight, while Iran targeted US sites in Bahrain, Kuwait ⁠and Jordan and a tanker was hit in ⁠the strait of Hormuz at the mouth of the Gulf. Two oil tankers carrying Saudi crude to Asia reversed course in the Red Sea on Tuesday after threats from Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis. Donald Trump said the Houthis had not yet shut the Bab el-Mandeb, the “Gate of Tears” strait leading ⁠into the Red Sea, and threatened to act against them if they did. “So far it hasn’t happened,” Trump said. “If something like that happens, we take care of it.” Trump renewed his threats to again attack Iran’s nuclear facilities at Natanz “pretty soon“, which he said in June 2025 had been “totally obliterated” after the US military bombed the facility. Defense secretary Pete Hegseth faced questions over the war with Iran, which he estimated has cost $37.5bn so far, as Republicans prepare a $95bn budget package to fund the military, along with other White House priorities. Trump pledged to help Lebanon “a lot” during a White House meeting with the country’s leader, Joseph Aoun. Trump made the surprise announcement that he’d allow American carriers to resume direct flights to Lebanon for the first time in 40-plus years after the US suspended the route. Oil prices gained more than 2% on Tuesday, with Brent crude hovering above $91 a barrel. The US defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, walked into the Senate on Tuesday to defend the Trump administration’s $87.6bn supplemental funding request, facing lawmakers furious that the US-Israel war on Iran is costing far more than the White House has admitted. Hegseth and the chair of the joint chiefs, Gen Dan Caine, faced the Senate appropriations committee, chaired by Susan Collins of Maine, as the Pentagon said it was running low on cash to keep the fight going. Of the total request, $67.1bn of the request is earmarked specifically to keep the war effort funded. It was the pair’s first sustained public questioning since a brief ceasefire collapsed and the war roared back to life. At least two US soldiers were killed in an Iranian attack on Friday, and another died in Iraq destroying an Iranian drone, pushing the American death toll since February to 17. Nearly 100 troops have been wounded in the conflict, officials confirm, though most injuries were minor. Iran’s top joint military command said it would tar
chineseTitle: 中东危机直播：运载沙特石油的油轮在胡塞威胁后返航；美国对伊朗发动更多打击
chineseSummary: 在给托运人的一封信中，胡塞武装威胁要攻击任何装卸沙特石油的船只
chineseContent: 关于美国袭击的更多细节：伊朗官方媒体称，德黑兰西部、东部和东北部听到了防空活动的声音。德黑兰的时间是凌晨 3.40。回顾今天早些时候的一些消息，唐纳德·特朗普表示，美国“很快”将袭击位于伊朗严重受损的纳坦兹铀浓缩设施附近的镐山地区。目前尚不清楚美国今天的袭击是否针对镐山。镐山是一个戒备森严的地点，有两个深埋的隧道综合体，专家评估它们超出了美国军火库中最强大的掩体炸弹的攻击范围。该网站与伊朗核计划有关。据伊朗官方媒体报道，伊朗军方领导人表示，任何针对核设施或“其他敏感设施”的攻击都将被视为重大升级。伊朗伊斯兰共和国武装部队哈塔姆·安比亚中央总部发表声明称：“我们宣布，如果该国的侵略和恐怖军队进入这样的阶段，我们将视其为整个地区战争的扩大，美国及其盟国及其支持者的所有利益都将成为伊朗伊斯兰共和国武装部队强力攻击的目标。”美国中央司令部宣布已连续11晚开始对伊朗军事目标进行打击。报告称，这些袭击的目的是继续削弱伊朗威胁霍尔木兹海峡商业航运的能力。随着我们了解更多信息，我们将为您提供有关这些罢工的更多详细信息。一名美国官员告诉美联社，在伊朗战争中受伤的美军人数已远远超过 500 人。总数高于五角大楼国防伤亡分析系统的官方统计数据，w
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
